last_kept_change.hypothesis: Runner-stability hardening becomes safer if any systems-optimizer card that declares `runner_focus`, `failure_mode`, `closed_fail_rule`, or `evidence_commands` is classified as runner-stability even when its id does not use the `sysopt.runner-stability-*` prefix, so naming drift cannot bypass replayable runner-law verification.
last_kept_change.measurement: Before the ratchet, `isRunnerStabilityRatchet` checked only `card.id.startsWith('sysopt.runner-stability')`, so kept cards such as `sysopt.runner-stale-cycle-exact-evidence-lock` and `sysopt.runner-observability-tail-lock` still carried `runner_focus`, `failure_mode`, `closed_fail_rule`, and `evidence_commands` yet bypassed `validateRunnerStabilityContract` because their ids drifted to `sysopt.runner-*` without the `-stability-` infix. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` classifies any card with those runner-stability fields as runner-stability, state policy explicitly requires that field-based classification, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
last_kept_change.keep_rule: Keep only if field-bearing runner cards are forced through the runner-stability contract even when their ids drift from `sysopt.runner-stability-*`, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
last_kept_change.learning_item: Runner-stability verification must classify by declared runner contract fields, not only by one id prefix; otherwise naming drift can let field-bearing recovery ratchets bypass the very laws they are supposed to prove.

last_rejected_change.hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card can only promote against a `gap_source_handoff` packet that resolves inside the current autonomous truth surfaces, so route-neutral system work cannot inherit XR evidence that exists only in an external runtime mirror.
last_rejected_change.measurement: Before the candidate ratchet, the XR control-plane contract only pinned `gap_source_handoff` as a string. Baseline falsification showed `test -f 'CLAW/control-plane/runtime/handoffs/C4-20260404T131253Z-04.json'` returned missing in this worktree and `test -f '/Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Website-main/CLAW/control-plane/runtime/truth-cache/packets/C4-20260403T165341Z-03.json'` also returned missing, while `test -f '/Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Website-main/CLAW/control-plane/runtime/handoffs/C4-20260404T131253Z-04.json'` still returned present in the external Website-main runtime mirror. Tightening `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` to require local or shared-cache handoff resolution would therefore turn the current XR ratchet inventory red before the bounded evaluation bundle even ran. The candidate was rejected instead of promoted.
last_rejected_change.keep_rule: Keep only if the authoritative `/work/xr` gap `source_handoff` resolves inside the current worktree runtime or the declared shared truth cache, and the tightened verifier plus fixed scope-local evaluation bundle pass without touching route implementation paths.
last_rejected_change.learning_item: XR handoff-resolution locks cannot promote until the authoritative `/work/xr` rejection packet is materialized inside the current worktree runtime or shared truth cache; today it only resolves through the external Website-main runtime mirror.

learning_log.learning: Runner-stability verification must classify by declared runner contract fields, not only by one id prefix; otherwise naming drift can let field-bearing recovery ratchets bypass the very laws they are supposed to prove.
Stale-cycle runner evidence must stay bound to the exact stale-lock proof pair; generic recovery replay is not specific enough to prove the stale-cycle surface.

XR severity baselines are exact four-level vectors, not loose maps; if extra or missing severity dimensions verify clean, route-neutral ratchets can drift away from the authoritative gap dimension system while still claiming baseline alignment.

XR severity locking must be declared in systems-optimizer state policy, not just enforced inside card validation or verifier summaries; otherwise the machine-level contract can drift from retained XR backlog law.

Seeded XR systems-optimizer ratchets must resolve their authoritative handoff packet in local runtime or shared truth cache before queue entry; otherwise route lanes can inherit XR work orders whose evidence only lives in an external mirror.

XR handoff-resolution locks cannot promote until the authoritative `/work/xr` rejection packet is materialized inside the current worktree runtime or shared truth cache; today it only resolves through the external Website-main runtime mirror.
Terminal systems-optimizer recency needs a singleton latest retained `learning_log.recorded_at` per status; if multiple ratchets share the newest second, terminal pointers stop being deterministic.

Seeded systems-optimizer slices need one exact UTC `recorded_at` shared across backlog, active state, and hypothesis card artifacts; otherwise queued or resumed recovery can silently retimestamp the same replayable ratchet.

XR systems-optimizer ratchets must lock `first_broken_law` to the authoritative primary drift surface; allowing any lower-ranked XR surface keeps route-neutral system work pointed away from the first measured failure.
Systems-optimizer `recorded_at` fields must use exact UTC ISO-8601 timestamps; recency locks are only deterministic when every retained artifact shares one canonical sortable clock format.
Systems-optimizer command lists must reject lone `&` shell operators; blocking `&&` and `||` alone still leaves backgrounded or chained shell work inside replayable proof.
Systems-optimizer command lists must stay shell-safe single commands; root-relative prefixes alone do not prevent chained shell steps from widening replayable proof.
Terminal systems-optimizer pointers must track the latest retained verdict per status; contract-aligned older pointers still make recovery and reporting stale.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must pin whether flagship invariants are currently intact; evaluator focus alone still lets route-neutral work misdiagnose shared-lane XR failure as IMC isolation drift.

The GGD binding must expose a repo-local lawset index as a first-class contract surface; relying only on external example-lawset pointers leaves equation truth partly implicit and lets missing local registry surfaces verify clean.
Runner-stability evidence commands must stay unique; duplicate recovery or observability steps make the replayable proof trail ambiguous without adding new evidence.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets need an exact handoff-evidence binding; route, law, and severity locks still allow stale control-plane work if the authoritative gap packet is refreshed.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets need a machine-readable shared quality baseline; evaluator focus alone leaves quality drift as narrative rather than falsifiable contract.
Runner-stability observability must stay trailing after the full replayable evidence trail; allowing `claw:health` mid-sequence weakens closed-fail recovery proof.

Systems-optimizer hypothesis-card inventory must stay backlog-linked; orphan or filename/id-drifted card files weaken replayable recovery and reporting by leaving stale artifacts outside backlog truth.
Systems-optimizer writable_scope entries must stay normalized repo-relative patterns; raw allowlist matching alone can hide dot-segment escapes into runtime or route surfaces.
Historical kept systems-optimizer cards must preserve the exact promotion bundle, or replayable history can silently widen the proof surface after promotion.
Active systems-optimizer slices need replayable seeded hypothesis-card artifacts; backlog alignment alone leaves interrupted recovery exposed to missing or drifted card files.
Terminal systems-optimizer verdicts need replayable hypothesis-card artifacts; state/backlog alignment alone still leaves recovery and reporting blind if the card file is missing.
Rejected systems-optimizer ratchets must mirror backlog contract fields in state, or recovery can carry stale failure history that no longer matches the ratchet actually rejected.
Scope-local promotion bundles must stay exact for promotable systems-optimizer cards; superset or reordered bundles silently widen the proof surface beyond the bounded slice.
Runner-stability evidence commands must stay inside an explicit root-relative runner boundary; arbitrary shell steps inside `evidence_commands` weaken replayability and closed-fail recovery proof.
Non-null active_hypothesis must stay backlog-linked and contract-identical to one seeded card, or interrupted systems-optimizer recovery can resume with silent scope or proof drift.
Systems-optimizer salvage commands must retain exact singleton group boundaries; flatten-equivalent merged groups weaken required proofs into alternatives.
Runner-stability observability must trail replayable runner evidence; `claw:health` can summarize recovery state but cannot lead the proof surface.
Runner-stability evidence must include at least one replayable runner script command; `claw:health` alone is observability, not recovery proof.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must use the exact evaluator focus contract; allowing extra or duplicate focus surfaces reintroduces vague route-neutral drift.
Systems-optimizer writable_scope must stay on the explicit control-plane allowlist; recursive control-plane globs are too broad because they can silently reach runtime and recovery state.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must pin the current severity baseline from the authoritative gap artifact, or route-neutral work can optimize against stale failure magnitude.

Systems-optimizer salvage must front-load the full scope-local promotion bundle before `claw:validate` or `claw:health`, or stale integration truth can preempt local contract drift and GGD law checks.
Resolved systems-optimizer backlog cards must retain the exact learning_item logged in state and learning_log, or recovery can replay one ratchet with split lessons across surfaces.
Systems-optimizer state must link back to unique backlog ids, verdict counts, and retained learning entries, or recovery can treat stale ratchet history as valid.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must bind to one named broken law from the authoritative gap surface list, or evaluator tightening still leaves route-neutral work too vague to falsify.
XR systems-optimizer ratchets must fail closed at backlog time; if the authoritative gap binding is optional before execution, route lanes can inherit vague XR work orders.
Runner-stability ratchets must reduce to one named failure surface with an explicit closed-fail rule and replayable evidence commands; multi-surface runner hardening language is too vague to promote.

Systems-optimizer recovery must self-verify its own state and backlog contract before replay or salvage, and that safety check has to promote through the scope-local bundle rather than stale-truth-sensitive global health gates.

Recurring XR ratchets must cite the authoritative `work-xr.geometry-gap` artifact and fixed evaluator focus, or the machine keeps optimizing around vague route-family language instead of the first broken law.
Systems-optimizer promotion bundles must stay scope-local; global health commands belong to runner observability, not keep-discard criteria for control-plane-only ratchets.

Any systems-optimizer slice that can mutate `GGD/**` must carry both binding verification and executable equation-law checks in its fixed evaluation bundle, or the machine can drift its truth layer without proving law integrity.
Runner-policy completeness ratchets cannot promote while `claw:validate` and `claw:health` are already red for stale integration truth outside the systems-optimizer writable slice.
Optimizer ratchets need explicit measurement and keep rules in backlog plus a retained learning_item in state, or repeated XR/autonomy failures remain under-specified.
Systems-optimizer bundles must be executable from the lane worktree root; otherwise autonomy and salvage loops fail for packaging reasons before they reach the real control-plane checks.
Resolved systems-optimizer history must keep replayable hypothesis-card artifacts for every kept/rejected card; terminal pointers alone leave older ratchets unverifiable.
Seeded systems-optimizer backlog items need replayable hypothesis-card artifacts before activation; otherwise queued recovery work can drift before state.active_hypothesis is populated.
